features.title: 31 MCP Tools
features.description: A comprehensive production toolset: memory search, agent spawning, text embedding, vector operations, pipeline execution, authentication, budget tracking, swarm coordination, and more — all typed, documented, and versioned.

features.title: Dual Transport (JSON-RPC + SSE)
features.description: HeadyMCP supports both synchronous JSON-RPC 2.0 and streaming Server-Sent Events transports in a single server implementation. Use synchronous calls for quick tool execution, streaming for real-time agent output pipelines.

features.title: Server Registry
features.description: A curated registry of Heady™ MCP servers, each with capability manifests, transport declarations, and auto-discovery metadata. Clients discover available tools and servers at runtime without manual configuration.

features.title: Zero-Latency Dispatch
features.description: Tool requests are dispatched at the Cloudflare edge — routing to the nearest worker with sub-10ms average dispatch latency globally. No cold starts. No regional bottlenecks. Just fast tool execution.

features.title: Type-Safe APIs
features.description: Every MCP tool is defined with full TypeScript typings, Zod schema validation, and auto-generated OpenAPI specs. Build against HeadyMCP with complete type safety from client SDK to server handler.

features.title: Auto-Discovery
features.description: MCP clients connecting to HeadyMCP automatically receive a capabilities manifest listing all available tools, their input/output schemas, transport requirements, and versioning. No hardcoded configs — dynamic at runtime.

features.title: Vertical Catalogs
features.description: Tools are organized into vertical catalogs: Memory Tools, Agent Tools, Vector Tools, Pipeline Tools, Auth Tools, and Monitoring Tools — making it easy to import only the capabilities your application needs.

features.title: Claude & OpenAI Compatible
features.description: HeadyMCP is fully compatible with Anthropic's Claude tool-use protocol and OpenAI's function calling specification. One MCP server implementation works seamlessly across both AI providers without modification.
